100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On March 30, 1926, the U.S. Supreme Court heard arguments in a significant case regarding the rights of labor unions, hinting at potential shifts in labor law. In Europe, British Prime Minister Stanley Baldwin addressed the House of Commons, urging support for a new budget amid economic challenges post-World War I. Meanwhile, in Africa, reports emerged of a devastating drought affecting regions in Sudan, raising concerns for food security and humanitarian aid.
